description: Server-side template injection (SSTI) anti-patterns: user input reaching a template compiler, double-render pipelines, sandbox escapes, and template-driven resource exhaustion.

anti_patterns.rule: User input concatenated / interpolated into a template SOURCE string that is then compiled (Template('Hi '+name), f-string or String.format feeding the engine). Bind values as context instead.
Template body loaded from a user-writable source (DB field, profile name, subject line, CMS block, request param) — the stored text is attacker code.

Engine output re-parsed and executed as a template a second time; a user value inert on pass one executes on pass two. Do not re-parse rendered output, or neutralise {{ }} / {% %} / ${ } in user fields before the second pass.
Untrusted authors given the raw engine without a sandbox + attribute allowlist; SSTI escalates to RCE via __class__/__globals__/getClass()/constructor traversal.
Autoescape disabled on a template rendering user data — same input path as SSTI, immediate XSS.

User-controlled loop / range / include / repeat count with no iteration or output-size cap — one request renders unbounded output and OOMs the renderer (a crash before a queue-offset commit can crash-loop).
